body:0001.source: This article is machine translated from english
body:0001.target: This article is machine translated from english

body:0003.source: Access rights control who can see content, who can participate, and who can manage projects or folders on the platform.
body:0003.target: Access rights control who can see content, who can participate, and who can manage projects or folders on the platform.

body:0004.source: The platform uses a three‑level system:
body:0004.target: The platform uses a three‑level system:

body:0005.source: Folder level: controls access and management of groups of projects
body:0005.target: Folder level: controls access and management of groups of projects

body:0006.source: Project level: determines discoverability and visibility, it also allows you to assign project managers
body:0006.target: Project level: determines discoverability and visibility, it also allows you to assign project managers

body:0007.source: Phase level: sets who can participate in each phase (e.g., posting, voting, surveys)
body:0007.target: Phase level: sets who can participate in each phase (e.g., posting, voting, surveys)

body:0010.source: Target consultations to the right audiences
body:0010.target: Target consultations to the right audiences

body:0011.source: Collect demographic data only when needed
body:0011.target: Collect demographic data only when needed

body:0014.source: Folder managers can edit folder pages, update descriptions, and oversee all projects in the folder
body:0014.target: Folder managers can edit folder pages, update descriptions, and oversee all projects in the folder

body:0015.source: A folder manager can act like a platform admin within the context of the folder
body:0015.target: A folder manager can act like a platform admin within the context of the folder

body:0017.source: A published folder is visible to everyone
body:0017.target: A published folder is visible to everyone

body:0018.source: If all projects in a folder are in draft, the folder stays hidden until at least one project is published
body:0018.target: If all projects in a folder are in draft, the folder stays hidden until at least one project is published

body:0019.source: If published projects are added to a draft folder, the folder won’t appear on the homepage, but the projects remain accessible via direct links or search
body:0019.target: If published projects are added to a draft folder, the folder won’t appear on the homepage, but the projects remain accessible via direct links or search

body:0022.source: Not every project needs to be fully visible on the platform. For example, you might want to keep a project private and share it only via a direct link. You can select how discoverable the project is:
body:0022.target: Not every project needs to be fully visible on the platform. For example, you might want to keep a project private and share it only via a direct link. You can select how discoverable the project is:

body:0024.source: Visible on the platform according to the settings in the Visibility section (see “Visibility” below).
body:0024.target: Visible on the platform according to the settings in the Visibility section (see “Visibility” below).

body:0025.source: Appears on the homepage and in widgets
body:0025.target: Appears on the homepage and in widgets

body:0027.source: Only visible to people who have the direct link
body:0027.target: Only visible to people who have the direct link

body:0028.source: Does not appear on the homepage or in widgets
body:0028.target: Does not appear on the homepage or in widgets

body:0029.source: Will not be indexed by search engines
body:0029.target: Will not be indexed by search engines

body:0030.source: Email notifications are sent only to participants
body:0030.target: Email notifications are sent only to participants

body:0031.source: This lets you control whether your project is public to everyone or shared privately with selected users.
body:0031.target: This lets you control whether your project is public to everyone or shared privately with selected users.

body:0033.source: Once you’ve set how discoverable your project is, you can define who can see it. There are three options:
body:0033.target: Once you’ve set how discoverable your project is, you can define who can see it. There are three options:

body:0034.source: Everyone: Anyone on the platform can view the project
body:0034.target: Everyone: Anyone on the platform can view the project

body:0035.source: Only admins and project managers: Restricted to platform admins and project managers that are managing the project
body:0035.target: Only admins and project managers: Restricted to platform admins and project managers that are managing the project

body:0036.source: A selected group: Limit visibility to a specific user group (see our article User Management and User Groups for more information on user grouping)
body:0036.target: A selected group: Limit visibility to a specific user group (see our article User Management and User Groups for more information on user grouping)

body:0038.source: As an admin, you can assign one or more project managers to a project. Project managers have the same rights as admins, but only within the scope of that specific project (e.g., they can update the project name, change settings, and more).
body:0038.target: As an admin, you can assign one or more project managers to a project. Project managers have the same rights as admins, but only within the scope of that specific project (e.g., they can update the project name, change settings, and more).

body:0039.source: In order to assign a project to a project manager, they first have to be added as a project manager to the platform.
body:0039.target: In order to assign a project to a project manager, they first have to be added as a project manager to the platform.

body:0041.source: Phase-level rights define who can participate in each phase action (posting input, commenting, voting, completing surveys, etc.). Commenting in particular always requires authentication: a participant must at least confirm their email or create an account, so the None (no-login) option does not enable anonymous, non-logged-in comments.
body:0041.target: Phase-level rights define who can participate in each phase action (posting input, commenting, voting, completing surveys, etc.). Commenting in particular always requires authentication: a participant must at least confirm their email or create an account, so the None (no-login) option does not enable anonymous, non-logged-in comments.

body:0043.source: Administrators & project managers: Only those with management rights can participate.
body:0043.target: Administrators & project managers: Only those with management rights can participate.

body:0044.source: None (only available to surveys, collection of ideas and proposals): Visitors can answer surveys without logging in.
body:0044.target: None (only available to surveys, collection of ideas and proposals): Visitors can answer surveys without logging in.

body:0045.source: ⚠️ Selecting this option may result in duplicate answers because there is no login/IP tracking
body:0045.target: ⚠️ Selecting this option may result in duplicate answers because there is no login/IP tracking

body:0046.source: Submissions remain anonymous (a unique submission ID is provided to the user).
body:0046.target: Submissions remain anonymous (a unique submission ID is provided to the user).

body:0047.source: Delayed registration: If None is selected as a prerequisite to participate, users can participate without registering. After submission, they will be prompted to register to stay informed.
body:0047.target: Delayed registration: If None is selected as a prerequisite to participate, users can participate without registering. After submission, they will be prompted to register to stay informed.

body:0048.source: Email confirmation: Residents only confirm their email to participate.
body:0048.target: Email confirmation: Residents only confirm their email to participate.

body:0049.source: No username is set by the user; the system auto-generates one (admins can choose “User” format or “Animal” format under Settings → General)
body:0049.target: No username is set by the user; the system auto-generates one (admins can choose “User” format or “Animal” format under Settings → General)

body:0050.source: Account creation: Full registration required (name, email, password)
body:0050.target: Account creation: Full registration required (name, email, password)

body:0051.source: SSO verification (if plan supports): Residents verify identity via SSO before participating; admins can set re‑verification frequency for high‑stakes projects
body:0051.target: SSO verification (if plan supports): Residents verify identity via SSO before participating; admins can set re‑verification frequency for high‑stakes projects

body:0052.source: ⚠️ Remember that if you are testing access rights and you are logged in as an admin you will not be able to see your changes because admins have the rights to override settings. To test your access rights make sure to log in using a user account to be able to recreate the user experience correctly.
body:0052.target: ⚠️ Remember that if you are testing access rights and you are logged in as an admin you will not be able to see your changes because admins have the rights to override settings. To test your access rights make sure to log in using a user account to be able to recreate the user experience correctly.

body:0054.source: Admins can limit phase participation to certain user groups (those users must already have project visibility). For learning more about user groups, see our article: User Management and User Groups
body:0054.target: Admins can limit phase participation to certain user groups (those users must already have project visibility). For learning more about user groups, see our article: User Management and User Groups

body:0056.source: You can configure the level of anonymity for participants through three options:
body:0056.target: You can configure the level of anonymity for participants through three options:

body:0057.source:
Include personally identifiable and demographic data:
All available user data will be stored and displayed, including first name, last name, and email address.
body:0057.target:
Include personally identifiable and demographic data:
All available user data will be stored and displayed, including first name, last name, and email address.

body:0058.source:
Exclude personally identifiable data but include demographic data:
Only demographic information (e.g., age group, location, department) will be shown. Personal details such as first name, last name, and email will not be stored.
Since participation cannot be linked to a specific user, participants will not be able to view their submission after participating.
body:0058.target:
Exclude personally identifiable data but include demographic data:
Only demographic information (e.g., age group, location, department) will be shown. Personal details such as first name, last name, and email will not be stored.
Since participation cannot be linked to a specific user, participants will not be able to view their submission after participating.

body:0059.source:
Exclude both personally identifiable and demographic data (full anonymity):
No personal or demographic information is stored or displayed. This option ensures complete anonymity of all participants. Since participation cannot be linked to a specific user, participants will not be able to view their submission after participating.
body:0059.target:
Exclude both personally identifiable and demographic data (full anonymity):
No personal or demographic information is stored or displayed. This option ensures complete anonymity of all participants. Since participation cannot be linked to a specific user, participants will not be able to view their submission after participating.

body:0061.source: Admins can enable extra registration questions for participants.
body:0061.target: Admins can enable extra registration questions for participants.

body:0062.source: Account creation: All registration questions are enabled by default (can be made required or optional)
body:0062.target: Account creation: All registration questions are enabled by default (can be made required or optional)

body:0063.source: Email confirmation: No questions by default, but admins can add any in the Extra Questions section
body:0063.target: Email confirmation: No questions by default, but admins can add any in the Extra Questions section

body:0064.source: Only admins (not project managers) can edit or create registration questions (done in Platform Settings → Registration). For more information read our article:
body:0064.target: Only admins (not project managers) can edit or create registration questions (done in Platform Settings → Registration). For more information read our article:

body:0065.source: Setting up the registration process and registration questions
body:0065.target: Setting up the registration process and registration questions

body:0066.source: Responses are stored in user profiles and visible in:
body:0066.target: Responses are stored in user profiles and visible in:

body:0068.source: Results section of the phase where questions were asked
body:0068.target: Results section of the phase where questions were asked
